questions: Keep things measured and level-headed. You just got a call saying you passed the bar exam on your first attempt after months of grueling preparation — a friend is texting asking if you want to celebrate tonight. What do you say back?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just found out your short story was accepted for publication in a journal you've been submitting to for three years — the editor is emailing to ask for your author bio. What do you send?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You've been trying to get pregnant for two years and the test just came back positive for the first time — your partner is asking what you want to do to mark the moment. How do you respond?
Keep things measured and level-headed. You just got off the phone with your dream employer offering you the job — they're asking you to confirm your start date by end of day. What do you write back?

Keep things measured and level-headed. Your band's song just got picked up by a popular podcast with millions of listeners — the host emailed asking if you'd like to record a short intro for it. What do you say?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just found out your visa application was approved — you've been waiting six months and had almost given up. The confirmation email asks you to schedule an appointment to pick up your documents. What do you do?

Keep things measured and level-headed. After eighteen months of searching, you just signed the offer on your first home — your real estate agent is asking if you want a photo by the door for the file. How do you respond?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just came offstage from the best performance of your life — the audience gave a standing ovation and your director is backstage asking how you feel about the show. What do you tell them?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You've been trying to get your parents into the same city for years, and your sibling just texted to say they're both moving within the month — they're asking if you want to start looking for a place to gather everyone. What do you say?

Keep things measured and level-headed. The funding proposal you wrote for your research project just came back approved at the full amount — your department chair is asking you to send a brief statement for the department newsletter. What do you write?
